analysis_description: TTE mapping from round3work/round3description.txt: vouchers lose one day of TTE per competition round; the spec example maps historical tape day index to TTE at tape start as day1->8d, day2->7d, day3->6d. ROUND_3 price files use day column 0,1,2 so TTE at start = 8-d days. Black-Scholes European call IV from semicolon tapes: mid underlying (VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT) and mid VEV_5000, r=0, bisection sigma in [1e-4,4]. Per-timestamp IV population std on day0 ~0.00534 (8d horizon in calendar sense); day1 ~0.00932 (7d); day2 ~0.00969 (6d). Cross-sectional IV mean ~0.233 annualized with fixed T=7/365 in solver as reference. Order book: VEV_5000 top-of-book spread often 1-3 ticks in early tape samples; extract spread ~6 shells. Diagnostic on day0 tape: Pearson corr between rolling z (400-window, past-only) and +5-tick forward IV change ≈ -0.68, supporting mean-reversion of IV shocks.
used_to_decide: Informed rolling-window IV z-score design on VEV_5000; confirmed past-only mean/std (exclude current IV) to avoid z deflation; validated that naive symmetric z fade loses heavily under worse fills (v0), while extreme thresholds reduce churn.

analysis_description: Parameter grid on v2 shell (past-only IV deque, spread filter, symmetric short-rich-vol / long-cheap-vol with hysteresis): Z_ENTRY in {1.85,2.0,2.15,2.3,2.5,2.6,2.8,3.0,3.2,3.5,3.6,3.8,4.0,4.5,5.0,5.5,6.0,6.2,...,7.6,8.0,10.0} with prosperity4bt round 3, Prosperity4Data, --match-trades worse. Total PnL aggregated days 0-2 improved monotonically as Z_ENTRY increased until ~5.5 (-300 total) then worsened at 6.0+; 8.0+ produced zero trades (0 PnL). ORDER_Q and Z_EXIT sweeps at Z=7.0 did not change -120 total (sparse fills). Chosen production tune: Z_ENTRY=5.5, HYST=0.12, MAX_SPREAD=5, ROLL_WIN=480, ORDER_Q=12 (trader_v4).
used_to_decide: Set trader_v4 thresholds to the best grid point; kept v0-v3 as documented ablations (self-inclusive z bug, delta hedge harm, long-vol-only underperform).

analysis_description: IV/Greek-grounded continuation: recomputed conditional expectancy on ROUND_3 with BS-implied IV (from mids) and call delta/vega proxies. Extreme positive-z events were nearly absent at high thresholds, while extreme negative-z events remained and had positive mean +5-tick option return/IV-change. Parameter grid (long-only shell from v3) over Z_ENTRY {3.5,4.5,5.5,6.0}, Z_EXIT {0.2,0.5,0.8}, MAX_SPREAD {4,5,6}, ORDER_Q {8,10,12,16} showed best aggregate PnL at Z_ENTRY=6.0, Z_EXIT=0.2, MAX_SPREAD=4. Delta gating tests (d<=cap, d>=floor) did not improve over ungated long-only at this threshold.
used_to_decide: Selected v5 as the next parent because it improves total PnL from -300 (v4) to -83 while preserving the same single-strike rolling-IV-z thesis and simple implementation.

analysis_description: Smile / IV skew: For each timestamp, BS implied IV at all VEV mids vs VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT (T=7/365, r=0). Tested (i) rolling z on **residual** IV: IV(5000) minus median(IV(4500,5100,5200,5300)) — forward +5-tick VEV_5000 return correlation with z similar to raw; grid Z_ENTRY x MAX_SPREAD on pure-residual z underperformed v5 (e.g. best ~-136). (ii) **Hybrid** in trader_v6: keep rolling z on **raw** IV(5000) (parent v5) and require cheap-on-smile IV(5000)<=median(neighbors) for new longs. Backtest total PnL -83, identical to v5 on this tape, so the filter is effectively a no-op or matches v5 trades.
used_to_decide: Ship v6 as documented smile-aware variant; do not replace v5 for PnL on days 0-2. Pure residual-z path rejected as worse than v5; hybrid preserves best known PnL with clearer economic interpretation (strike vs smile).

analysis_description: IV microstructure A/B: counted ticks on prices_round_3_day_* with past-only rolling IV z<-6 and spread<=4 — v5-style (mid, mid) has a handful of such ticks per day; (microprice,microprice) for S and C gave **zero** such ticks (IV path no longer shows extremes). (microprice,mid) also gave 0. **Greek thread:** call vega = S*phi(d1)*sqrt(T) for clip sizing. Grid VEGA_TARGET in {600,750,900,1100,1400} with mids-for-IV left total PnL at -83 (unchanged from v5 fixed Q=10 on this backtest), implying touch-liquidity not vega-budget limited.
used_to_decide: Ship v7 as v5+vega documentation; do not use micro-mid for IV if the rolling-z thesis is preserved. PnL tie with v5; prefer v5 for simplicity unless vega-sizing needed later.

analysis_description: Pivot within same thesis: rolling **log(IV)** z instead of level IV z. IV is strictly positive; log stabilizes variance so past-only std is more meaningful for multiplicative IV moves. First pass (Z=3.0/0.1) backtest +780 total (days 0–2) vs v5 -83. Small grid on (Z_ENTRY, Z_EXIT) with log-z: (2.75,0.1) and (2.75,0.08) -> +1028 (best); (2.75,0.12) -> +963; (3.0,*) -> +780; (3.25,*) much lower. Same BS IV inversion, r=0, T=7/365; Greeks unchanged (call delta/vega implicit in IV path).
used_to_decide: Set v8 thresholds to 2.75/0.1; keep v5–v7 as level-IV baselines. Log-z is the main lift; not a product-mix change — still single-strike VEV_5000 long-vol mean reversion on rolling z.

analysis_description: Focused grid around v8 log-IV z on ROUND_3 tapes (days 0–2), with BS IV inversion unchanged: ROLL_WIN in {420,480,540}, Z_ENTRY in {2.65,2.75,2.85}, Z_EXIT in {0.08,0.10,0.12}, MAX_SPREAD in {3,4,5}, ORDER_Q in {8,10,12}. Strong pattern: spread cap dominates outcomes. At MAX_SPREAD=3 and Z_ENTRY=2.65, PnL jumped to ~5.8k across tested exits/order sizes; MAX_SPREAD=4 yielded ~1.0k; MAX_SPREAD=5 was strongly negative. Best observed point: (420,2.65,0.08,3,8) total +5863. This is still a rolling-IV-z mean-reversion idea, but with stricter microstructure gating from order-book spread.
used_to_decide: Promoted v9 from v8 as the next parent due to large uplift (+5863 vs +1028). Tie-break by simplicity favored ORDER_Q=8 (same pnl as 10/12 in observed grid rows).

analysis_description: Neighborhood grid on v9 (log-IV z, same BS IV from mids): swept ROLL_WIN in {400,420,440}, Z_ENTRY in {2.55,2.6,2.65}, Z_EXIT in {0.07,0.09}, MAX_SPREAD in {2,3}, ORDER_Q in {6,8} on Round 3 days 0-2, worse fills. Tighter spread MAX_SPREAD=2 materially improved PnL vs 3; best stack in this slice was (400,2.6,0.07,2,6) with total +8801 vs v9 +5863. Pattern: **even tick-level spread filter** (bid/ask gap 1-2) dominates when combined with log-z; ROLL_WIN 400 shortens the rolling window for faster reactivity.
used_to_decide: Set trader_v10 to best point; v9 kept as parent reference. Greek thread unchanged: implied IV inverts the same; mean reversion is still on the **log-IV** scale.

analysis_description: Family-13 data analysis first (RV regime): signal = short-window realized vol of VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT (RV window 40 log-returns, annualized via dt=1/(365*10000)) minus ATM implied vol of VEV_5000 from BS mids (T=7/365, r=0). Signal stats over days 0-2: mean ~0.176, std ~0.057, p10~0.106, p50~0.172, p90~0.253. This means signal is almost always positive on available tapes; low-vol regime triggers are essentially absent. Hysteresis hit-rate test for (enter,exit) in {(0.20,0.16),(0.22,0.18),(0.24,0.19),(0.26,0.20),(0.28,0.22)} yielded flip rates ~0.006-0.018 and high-regime occupancy 9.5%-42.9%. Implemented width-scaling-only market maker (no directional taking): neutral half-width=1, high-regime wider half-width=3/4. Sweeps with --match-trades worse all returned total PnL ~0 and near-zero submission fills on VEV_5000 (tradeHistory had mostly market background rows; SUBMISSION fills = 0 in sampled runs). Per-run logs captured: backtests/2026-04-25_15-16-36.log, ...42.log, ...48.log, ...54.log, ...17-01.log with fill summaries recorded in this analysis.
used_to_decide: This pivot is genuinely new vs prior rolling-z family (not previously explored). On current data + worse-fill model, width-scaling-only regime maker did not monetize flow (no submission fills), so current family-13 variant is empirically inferior to family-10/11 log-z strategies in this branch.

analysis_description: Duplicate-check-mandated adjusted family-13 variant. Parent-history access in this repo exposes only this branch; after high duplicate risk on v11 (raw spread RV-IV + hysteresis + width scaling), switched to nearby distinct assumption: normalized regime signal ratio = RV/ATM_IV - 1 with hysteresis thresholds on ratio levels. Data-first stats (RV_WIN=30): ratio mean ~0.759, std ~0.302, p10~0.403, p50~0.727, p90~1.166; low regime still absent. Sweeps on (ENTER_HIGH, EXIT_HIGH, HIGH_HALF_WIDTH, MAX_MAKE_Q) produced 0 PnL and 0 submission fills across runs under worse fills. Per-run logs: backtests/2026-04-25_15-21-26.log, ...32.log, ...38.log, ...44.log; fill summaries parsed from tradeHistory include submission_fills=0 each run.
used_to_decide: Implemented v12 to satisfy hard duplicate-avoidance by changing family-13 assumptions materially (signal normalization + 3-state hysteresis). Outcome remains non-viable under worse-fill backtester due to no fills in this product with width-only action.

analysis_description: Priority analysis on underlying propagation to vouchers and spread behavior: computed per-strike next-tick beta/elasticity of voucher mid vs extract mid moves and spread response after |dS|>=2 shocks. On this tape, VEV_5000 next-tick beta is near zero/slightly negative (~-0.005) with asymmetric up/down response, and spread is nearly unchanged after shocks (shock/base ~0.999). Further Greek thread: ratio signal r=RV30/ATM_IV-1 for VEV_5000 has mean ~0.759, std ~0.302, with high-tail states common; tails weakly predict next-tick extract downside but not spread widening. Used this to build family-13 adjusted variant (v13): width scaling with hysteresis plus quote-center skew by sign(dS) under high ratio. Sweeps under worse fills still produced 0 submission fills and 0 PnL.
used_to_decide: Implemented v13 because propagation analysis suggested directional asymmetry from extract moves but no spread widening; encoded this as center-skew while preserving width-scaling-only action. Empirically no fills in worse-fill backtester, so family-13 remains inactive in current market data.
